title: International GCSE Demo Science (9000)
summary: This synthetic qualification is included for offline demonstration only.
It is designed to show the guide layout, validation gates, and source-traceable workflow without downloading copyrighted specification files.
International GCSEs are linear qualifications, so the demo guide models end-of-course revision.

topics.title: Measurement and data
points: Use SI units and standard prefixes
Record measurements to an appropriate precision
Calculate mean values and identify anomalous results
Represent data in tables and graphs

source_snippets.text: Demo specification page 4: students should use SI units, record measurements with suitable precision, calculate mean values, and represent data clearly in tables and graphs.

topics.title: Forces and motion
points: Describe speed as distance travelled per unit time
Apply simple force and motion relationships

source_snippets.text: Demo specification page 7: students should describe speed, interpret distance-time graphs, explain balanced and unbalanced forces, and apply simple force and motion relationships.

topics.title: Materials and change
points: Distinguish physical changes from chemical changes
Describe conservation of mass in closed systems
Use particle models to explain changes of state

source_snippets.text: Demo specification page 10: students should classify materials, distinguish physical and chemical changes, describe conservation of mass, and use particle models for changes of state.

assessments.title: Paper 1
details: Questions cover any part of the demo specification

source_snippets.text: Demo specification page 14: Paper 1 is 1 hour 30 minutes, has 90 marks, and contributes 50% of the qualification.

assessments.title: Paper 2
details: Questions include data handling and application

source_snippets.text: Demo specification page 15: Paper 2 is 1 hour 30 minutes, has 90 marks, and contributes 50% of the qualification.

audience_note: This offline demo models an OxfordAQA-style International GCSE guide for international students following a British curriculum outside the UK. It is a synthetic sample, not an official qualification.
